global.sidebarDesc: The left menu lists all CitéVision sections: dashboard, cameras, rules, alerts, and more. Each icon opens a dedicated page.
global.sidebarTip: On mobile, open the menu with the ☰ button at the top. On large screens you can collapse it to save space.
sidebarSteps: Find the groups: Surveillance, Analysis, Administration.
Click an entry to switch pages.
Use the bottom button to collapse or expand the menu.
global.searchDesc: Type a camera, rule, or alert name to jump there without browsing menus.
global.searchTip: Click the bar and type the first letters of what you need.
searchSteps: Click the search bar at the top.
Pick a result from the list.
global.themeDesc: Switch between dark mode (comfort) and light mode (daylight readability). Your choice is remembered.
global.themeTip: Tutorials and pop-ups adapt automatically to the active theme.
global.contentDesc: Page content appears here. A small ? button top-right replays the detailed tutorial for the current page.
global.contentTip: Read each step calmly: a 4-second pause helps you absorb the information.
contentSteps: Read the page title and subtitle.
Click ? anytime to replay the guide.

login.brandDesc: CitéVision is the intelligent video platform: cameras, zones, AI rules, and evidence-backed alerts. This page is only for signing in.
login.brandTip: Light/dark theme (top right) also applies to tutorials.
login.formDesc: Enter the email and password from your administrator. After sign-in you land on the demo center or dashboard.
login.formTip: If you see « server unreachable », check that the stack is running (scripts/start-linux.sh).
login.emailDesc: Unique account identifier. Must match exactly what is registered in CitéVision.
login.emailTip: Use the full email, not just a first name.
login.passwordDesc: Personal secret. Click the eye icon to verify typing without leaving it visible.
login.passwordTip: After repeated failures, ask an admin to reset your password.
login.submitDesc: Sends credentials to the server. If valid, you are redirected into the app.
login.submitTip: Never share your password over chat or email.

setup.brandDesc: This page appears only once when CitéVision is deployed on a fresh server. You create the organization and administrator account.
setup.brandTip: Save the admin password — you need it for the first sign-in.
setup.progressDesc: Three steps: organization name, admin account, summary before validation.
setup.progressTip: You can go back without losing what you already entered.
setup.step1Desc: Name shown in the UI (e.g. « City operations », « North warehouse »). You can change it later in Settings.
setup.step1Tip: Pick a name your team will recognize.
setup.step2Desc: Sign-in email and strong password (12+ chars, upper, lower, digit). This account has full rights.
setup.step2Tip: Confirm the password twice to avoid typos.
setup.step3Desc: Review organization and email before validating. After Finish you are redirected to sign-in.
setup.step3Tip: Setup fails if the backend server is not running.
step3Steps: Sign in with the same email and password.
setup.submitDesc: Moves to the next step or runs final creation on step 3.
setup.submitTip: A spinner means the server is working — please wait.

dashboard.statsTip: Hover each card for a short explanation.
statsSteps: Check how many cameras are active.
Confirm how many rules are enabled.
dashboard.alertsTip: Red or orange alerts often need a quick response.
alertsSteps: Click a row to open details.
dashboard.liveTip: Detections appear only when a camera streams and a rule is enabled.
liveSteps: Enable at least one rule in Rules.
dashboard.navAlertsDesc: Opens the full alerts page with filters, evidence, and acknowledgement.
dashboard.navAlertsTip: Use this when the dashboard summary is not enough.

map.modesTip: Start with “Plan” if you are new — simpler than the 3D globe.
modesSteps: Zoom with the wheel or + / − buttons.
Return to plan view to reposition a camera.
map.canvasTip: A misplaced map marker does not affect AI — only the video stream matters.
canvasSteps: Hover a marker for live preview.
Click to open the camera card.
Drag the marker to fix GIS position.

rules.catalogTip: Filter by scale (national, enterprise, domestic) for your context.
catalogSteps: Read the real / partial / beta badge.
Click Configure on the rule you need.
rules.activeTip: Only rules listed here are actually monitored by the engine.
activeSteps: Open a rule to edit zones or thresholds.
rules.helpBtnDesc: This ? button replays the full Rules page tutorial anytime.
rules.helpBtnTip: Handy after an update or when onboarding a teammate.

alerts.filtersTip: Combine filters to isolate one incident.
filtersSteps: Filter by camera or rule type.
Sort by severity if the list is long.
alerts.listDesc: Each row summarizes an incident: time, camera, triggered rule, and message. Alerts with evidence show a thumbnail.
alerts.listTip: Click an alert to open details and download evidence.
listSteps: Open the alert for clip and images.

videoWall.layoutTip: Start with 4 tiles — good balance of clarity and coverage.
layoutSteps: Go fullscreen on one tile if needed.
videoWall.gridTip: A black tile usually means offline camera or broken RTSP.
gridSteps: Check status on the Cameras page.
Swap in another camera if needed.

liveView.playerTip: The displayed stream is the same source analyzed by AI.
playerSteps: Select a camera in the list.
liveView.sidebarDesc: All cameras one click away. Online / offline status is shown with a colored dot.
liveView.sidebarTip: Camera names often include the site for easier scanning.

cameras.listTip: “Offline” may be a network issue, not always hardware failure.
listSteps: Verify IP address and RTSP port.
Check System Health if many cameras drop.
cameras.wizardTip: Have RTSP login and password ready before starting the wizard.
wizardSteps: Network scan or manual RTSP URL.

cameraWizard.introDesc: This 4-step assistant connects an IP camera (RTSP) to CitéVision. No advanced networking skills required — follow the steps in order.
cameraWizard.introTip: Keep manufacturer credentials handy (often admin / camera password).
introSteps: Step 1: find the camera on the network or enter URL.
cameraWizard.step1Desc: Enter the subnet (e.g. 192.168.1.0/24) and scan. Or switch to manual RTSP URL entry.
cameraWizard.step1Tip: Hikvision and Dahua often pre-fill the URL; check the manual for other brands.
cameraWizard.step2Desc: Enter the camera RTSP username and password. They are stored securely for continuous streaming.
cameraWizard.step2Tip: If the test fails, ensure the account has “video stream” rights on the camera UI.
cameraWizard.step3Desc: CitéVision tries to open the RTSP stream. Success means URL and credentials are correct.
cameraWizard.step3Tip: Common failure: wrong port (554 default) or RTSP disabled on the camera.
cameraWizard.step4Desc: Check the live image, give the camera a clear name, then confirm. It will appear in all lists and accept zones.
cameraWizard.step4Tip: Name by physical location (e.g. “North entrance”) for clearer alerts.

zones.toolbarDesc: Pick Zone (polygon) or Line (counting) mode, select the source camera, then save your drawings.
zones.toolbarTip: A poorly named zone may prevent the right AI rule from applying.
toolbarSteps: Switch Zone or Line as needed.
zones.canvasTip: Minimum 3 clicks for a zone; 2 clicks for a counting line.
canvasSteps: Click each polygon corner on the video.
Drag points to adjust if needed.
zones.behaviorDesc: Assign a semantic type (speed, counting, phone use, etc.). This determines which catalog rules can attach.
zones.behaviorTip: Menu options come from AI capabilities actually loaded on your server.
behaviorSteps: Read the description under each option.
Save then enable the matching rule.

events.filtersTip: Filter by type (speed, crossing, presence…) to find one incident.
filtersSteps: Select one or more event types.
Narrow the time range if the list is long.
events.timelineTip: Each event is a raw AI detection — not always a final alert.
timelineSteps: Click an event in the list.
Open evidence in the right panel.
events.detailDesc: The right panel shows the video clip (often 6 s), still images, and metadata (speed, object class, etc.).
events.detailTip: Missing clip or images may mean evidence policy was not met at detection time.

users.tableTip: Each role limits visible menu pages.
tableSteps: Disable rather than delete to keep history.
users.addDesc: Create an account with email, password, and role. The user gets permissions matching their profile.
users.addTip: Start with the least permissive role needed (least privilege).
addSteps: Click Add at the top right.

addUser.introDesc: Create an account for a new operator or viewer. Only administrators can add users.
addUser.introTip: The temporary password should be changed on first login if your policy requires it.
introSteps: Enter full name (shown in audit logs).
Pick the role that matches the job.
addUser.name: Full name
addUser.nameDesc: First and last name as shown in audit logs and user lists.
addUser.nameTip: Avoid nicknames: prefer « Jane Doe » over « JD ».
addUser.emailDesc: Used as sign-in ID. Each email can be used only once in the organization.
addUser.emailTip: Check spelling: a typo blocks sign-in.
addUser.passwordDesc: Minimum 12 characters. The user can change it under Settings → Profile after sign-in.
addUser.passwordTip: Use a password manager to generate a strong value.
passwordSteps: Share it over a secure channel.
Ask the user to change it quickly.
addUser.roleDesc: Admin: full access. Operator: cameras, rules, alerts. Viewer: read-only, no changes.
addUser.roleTip: A viewer cannot enable rules or archive alerts.
addUser.submitDesc: Saves the user immediately. They can sign in as soon as the server responds.
addUser.submitTip: If it fails, check whether the email already exists.

audit.logTip: Useful for security audits or disputes about operator actions.
logSteps: Filter by date if the log is large.
Find author and action (login, rule, camera…).
Export or copy for external reports.

health.servicesTip: Red = stopped or unreachable. Restart via indicated scripts or contact admin.
servicesSteps: Check Backend, AI engine, Rules engine, go2rtc.
Click a red service for instructions.
Wait 30 s after restart before retesting.
health.aiModelsTip: Without YOLO, no object detection; without PaddleOCR, no plate reading.
aiModelsSteps: Import a custom ONNX model if needed.
Restart the AI engine after import.
health.importModelDesc: Add a custom model (phone, seatbelt, etc.) to enrich zone behavior menus.
health.importModelTip: The file must be a real exported .onnx, not a placeholder.
importModelSteps: Confirm the model appears in System Health.

modelImport.introDesc: This wizard registers a custom AI model for your organization. Once imported, it appears in zone and line menus and in the capabilities catalog.
modelImport.introTip: Prepare the .onnx file or a direct HTTPS URL to the file.
modelImport.step1Desc: Templates pre-fill fields for phone use, seatbelt, etc. “Custom” leaves everything configurable.
modelImport.step1Tip: A matching template reduces configuration mistakes.
modelImport.step2Desc: Upload a .onnx from your computer or provide a public HTTPS link. The server validates format before install.
modelImport.step2Tip: Typical size: a few MB to hundreds of MB depending on the model.
modelImport.step3Desc: Model ID, MQTT event type, FR/EN labels, and scope (zone, line, or both). Shown in the operator UI.
modelImport.step3Tip: Use short IDs without spaces for the technical name.
modelImport.step4Desc: List output classes and which ones trigger an alert (positive classes).
modelImport.step4Tip: For binary classification: negative, positive is a good start.
modelImport.step5Desc: Summary before submit. On success, the backend asks the AI engine to reload secondary models.
modelImport.step5Tip: If reload fails, check AI engine logs (port 8001).
step5Steps: Verify the model is loaded in Health.

redLightAssistant.introDesc: This assistant guides you to configure two complementary zones on the same camera, then enable the red-light rule with evidence.
redLightAssistant.introTip: Without both zones, the rule cannot work correctly.
redLightAssistant.step1Desc: Draw a polygon on the traffic light in ZoneEditor and assign behavior traffic_light_color.
redLightAssistant.step1Tip: Cover only the light, not the whole intersection.
redLightAssistant.step2Desc: Draw the intersection zone (red_light_observation) where vehicles are monitored when the light is red.
redLightAssistant.step2Tip: Same camera as the light zone — otherwise correlation fails.
redLightAssistant.step3Desc: Once both zones show ✓ green, enable tpl-red-light with 6 s clip, 2 images, and plate reading.
redLightAssistant.step3Tip: The button stays disabled until a zone is missing.

confirmDialog.introDesc: This dialog asks for your approval before an important action: delete, disable, reset, etc.
confirmDialog.introTip: Always read the message before confirming — some actions cannot be undone.
confirmDialog.messageDesc: The title summarizes the action. The text explains consequences. A monospace line may name the affected item.
confirmDialog.messageTip: In danger mode (red), the action deletes or disables permanently.
confirmDialog.actionsDesc: Cancel closes the dialog with no change. Confirm runs the described action.
confirmDialog.actionsTip: If unsure, click Cancel — you can return later.

settings.tabsTip: Each tab groups a topic: appearance, profile, email, evidence, tutorials…
tabsSteps: Evidence & capture: clip length, image count.
settings.toursPanelDesc: Enable or disable mini-tutorials on all pages and pop-ups. You can also manually replay each guide.
settings.toursPanelTip: Disabled = no auto-start, but ? stays available when tutorials are enabled.
toursPanelSteps: Buttons below: replay a specific guide.

demo.bannerTip: All green chips = demo ready for a client presentation.
demo.videoDesc: Upload a test MP4 or pick a real camera. The LIVE badge confirms AI can analyze this stream.
demo.videoTip: Only one source is analyzed at a time — explicitly select the video you need.
videoSteps: Wait for conversion if the file is large.
Check the LIVE badge on the player.
demo.zonesDesc: Below the video, expand the zone panel: 3 clicks for a polygon, then Save. Enabled rules monitor these zones.
demo.zonesTip: Without a drawn zone, spatial rules (speed, counting…) cannot trigger.
zonesSteps: Expand the panel under the video.
demo.stepsDesc: This panel summarizes the demo: watch → draw zones → enable a rule → watch alerts and detections.
demo.stepsTip: Follow the numbers in order on a first presentation.
demo.rulesDesc: Pick an illustrated rule (speed, traffic light, counting, phone, seatbelt…) and click Configure to enable it on your zone.
demo.rulesTip: Each rule shows a badge for full (real) or partial support.
rulesSteps: Link the zone and complete the wizard.
demo.detectionsDesc: Raw AI events for the active video. Click a row for the premium preview (clip + images).
demo.detectionsTip: Allow 30–60 s after enabling a rule for first rows.
demo.alertsFeedDesc: Finalized alerts with evidence and emails (preview in MailHog). This is the business result of zone → AI → rule → proof.
demo.alertsFeedTip: No alert? Check that a rule is enabled and matches the zone.
alertsFeedSteps: Check MailHog for the alert email.

ruleActivation.introDesc: This wizard turns a catalog template into an active rule for your organization. Four steps: target, conditions, evidence, outputs.
ruleActivation.introTip: Draw the zone or line on the right camera before starting.
introSteps: Step 1: pick camera, zone, or line.
ruleActivation.step1Desc: Select the camera then the zone or line to monitor. Without a spatial link, the rule does not know where to analyze.
ruleActivation.step1Tip: The menu only offers zones compatible with the rule type.
step1Steps: Check the video preview on the right.
ruleActivation.step2Desc: Fine-tune thresholds (max speed, dwell time, object classes…). Defaults often work for a first activation.
ruleActivation.step2Tip: Use the logic simulator to see when the rule becomes true.
ruleActivation.step3Desc: View or edit the logic tree (AND / OR). Advanced mode for experienced operators.
ruleActivation.step3Tip: When unsure, keep the catalog default tree.
ruleActivation.evidenceDesc: Set video clip, image count, plate reading for road rules. A “complete” alert is sent only when these requirements are met.
ruleActivation.step4Desc: Choose email, webhook, severity, and cooldown. Click Activate — the rule appears in Active rules and starts monitoring.
ruleActivation.step4Tip: A long cooldown can make the rule feel inactive.

evidenceViewer.introDesc: This screen gathers all proof material for an alert or event: video clip, HD images, and metadata (plate, speed, etc.).
evidenceViewer.introTip: Evidence is captured at detection time, not reconstructed later.
introSteps: Read the summary banner at the top.
Play the clip to review the action.
Download or share if your role allows.
evidenceViewer.clipDesc: Short excerpt (often 6 seconds) centered on the detection moment. Controls support pause, seek, and fullscreen.
evidenceViewer.clipTip: Missing clip may mark the alert incomplete per policy.
evidenceViewer.imagesDesc: One or more high-resolution captures complement the clip to identify people, vehicles, or plates.

alertSeverity.title: Severity filter
alertSeverity.desc: Limits the list to Low, Medium, High, or Critical alerts. Helps prioritize urgent cases.
alertSeverity.tip: Critical = sound + red banner; Low = discreet notification.
steps: Pick a level or leave « All ».
Combine with a camera to target one site.
Open an alert to view evidence.

alertCamera.title: Camera filter
alertCamera.desc: Shows only alerts from one camera. Useful when several sites share one organization.
alertCamera.tip: Camera names often include the physical location (e.g. « North entrance »).
steps: Add a date range if history is long.

loginEmail.title: Sign-in email
loginEmail.desc: Your CitéVision account ID. Must match exactly what the administrator registered.
loginEmail.tip: Paste from the invitation email to avoid typos.

loginPassword.title: Password
loginPassword.desc: Personal secret. Never share it, even with someone claiming to be support.
loginPassword.tip: The eye icon lets you verify typing without leaving the password visible long.

userFullName.title: Display name
userFullName.desc: Full name shown in lists and the audit log.

userEmail.title: User email
userEmail.desc: Sign-in ID for the new account. Must be unique in the organization.

userPassword.title: Temporary password
userPassword.desc: Minimum 12 characters. Share securely with the new user.
userPassword.tip: Generate a random value with a password manager.
steps: Do not send in plain public SMS.

userRole.title: Role and permissions
userRole.desc: Admin = full setup. Operator = daily operations. Viewer = read-only.
userRole.tip: Least privilege reduces risk if an account is compromised.

zoneBehavior.title: Zone behavior
zoneBehavior.desc: This menu lists what the AI must compute inside the polygon: speed, counting, phone use, etc. Only options actually available on your server are shown.
zoneBehavior.tip: A greyed-out option means a required model is not loaded yet (see System Health).
steps: Pick the behavior that matches your rule.
Save the zone after any change.
Then activate the catalog rule that uses this behavior.

lineBehavior.title: Line behavior
lineBehavior.desc: For a counting line, select the event type emitted when someone or a vehicle crosses the line (count, speed, custom model…).
lineBehavior.tip: The list stays in sync with AI capabilities and ONNX models imported for your organization.
steps: Draw the line first with 2 clicks on the video.
Save then link a counting or crossing rule.

evidencePolicy.title: Evidence policy
evidencePolicy.desc: Defines what CitéVision must capture before sending a “complete” alert: clip length, still image count, plate read for road rules.
evidencePolicy.tip: Common default: 6 s clip + 2 images. Without a clip, the alert may stay partial.
steps: Enable clip and images per legal or operational needs.
Adjust duration for fast (speed) vs slow (intrusion) scenes.
Validate on a test alert in the Demo Center.

clipDuration.title: Clip duration
clipDuration.desc: Seconds recorded around the detection instant. The clip is centered on the event to show context before and after.
clipDuration.tip: 6 s fits most road offenses; 10–15 s for crowd scenes.
steps: Enter a value between 3 and 30 seconds.
Reduce if disk space is tight.

imageCount.title: Image count
imageCount.desc: Still captures besides the clip: overview (scene), close-up (subject), plate crop when available.
imageCount.tip: 2 images (scene + subject) are enough for most cases.
steps: Open an alert to check quality.

sequenceCondition.title: Sequence condition
sequenceCondition.desc: Requires several events in order or within a time window (e.g. presence then crossing).
sequenceCondition.tip: Advanced scenarios only; keep the catalog default if you are new.
steps: Read the plain-language summary under the tree.
Change only if the business scenario requires it.
Use the simulator to test logic.

outputChannels.title: Output channels
outputChannels.desc: How the alert is delivered: email, webhook to external systems, in-app notification.
outputChannels.tip: You can enable several channels in parallel for the same rule.
steps: Add a webhook if a ticket must be created automatically.
Test with MailHog in demo environments.

notifyEmail.title: Email notification
notifyEmail.desc: Address that receives a structured message with proof links when the rule fires.
notifyEmail.tip: Use a distribution list rather than a single person in production.
steps: Adjust the email template in Settings if needed.

catalogConfigure.title: Configure a rule
catalogConfigure.desc: Opens the 4-step wizard: target (camera/zone), conditions, evidence, then activation. The rule appears under Active Rules once saved.
catalogConfigure.tip: Draw the zone on the right camera first to unlock spatial fields.
steps: Click Configure on the desired card.
Follow the wizard without skipping steps.
Wait 30–60 s after activation for first alerts.

catalogNotConfigurable.title: Rule not configurable
catalogNotConfigurable.desc: This template needs a missing AI module, zone, or setting. The partial/beta badge or message explains what blocks setup.
catalogNotConfigurable.tip: Check System Health for unloaded models; import ONNX if needed.
steps: Read the reason on the card.
Return when the card becomes configurable again.

severity.title: Alert severity
severity.desc: Urgency level for operators: Low (subtle), Medium (badge), High (banner), Critical (continuous sound + push).
severity.tip: Reserve Critical for dangerous situations so alerts are not ignored.
steps: Pick the level that matches business risk.
Test visual and sound rendering on a fake alert.

evidenceForward.title: Forward evidence
evidenceForward.desc: Sends the proof package (clip, images, metadata) to an external channel after ack or manually.
evidenceForward.tip: Useful for legal archive or ERP; check GDPR compliance.
steps: Test on an alert with complete proof.

forwardEmail.title: Forward email
forwardEmail.desc: Address receiving a copy of the proof file. Can differ from the initial alert email.

forwardWebhook.title: Forward webhook
forwardWebhook.desc: HTTPS URL called with JSON metadata and links to proof files.
forwardWebhook.tip: The endpoint should respond within 5 s to avoid timeouts.
steps: Paste the URL from your IT team.

totp2fa.title: Two-factor authentication
totp2fa.desc: Adds a temporary code (TOTP) after your password. Works with Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.
totp2fa.tip: Keep backup codes; without your phone you cannot sign in.
steps: Scan the QR code with the app.

speed_limit_kmh.title: Speed limit
speed_limit_kmh.desc: Threshold in km/h above which the rule treats a vehicle as speeding in the calibrated zone.
speed_limit_kmh.tip: Calibrate zone edges (real length) first for reliable speeds.
steps: Enter the legal or internal limit.
Test with a known vehicle in demo.
Adjust if readings seem consistently high/low.

min_duration_sec.title: Minimum duration
min_duration_sec.desc: Minimum presence or offense time before triggering. Filters very short false positives.
min_duration_sec.tip: 2–3 s often enough for phone or seatbelt; more for parking.
steps: Increase if too many nuisance alerts.
Decrease if short offenses are missed.
